ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn class for a double chicken burger feast meal set; FOODON_00002383 (chicken sandwich food product) is the closest defensible parent class. The feast format including fries, drink, and pie is not individually represented.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Chicken burger) is the closest defensible match. The double-patty spicy tomato variant and feast meal set components are not individually coded in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for a commercial fast-food chicken burger feast meal set.

preparation_method: served as meal set with fries, drink, and pie
allergens: soy (likely in sauce and patty coating)

serving.description: 1 feast meal set (burger + medium fries + drink + pie)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is the complete feast meal set comprising a double chicken burger, medium fries, a beverage, and a pie, estimated at approximately 700 g total weight.

nutrient_source.title: Spicy Tomato Double Chicken Burger Feast — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values per 100 g are taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this product (cr_id G00010116). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1 (data not available).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific commercial feast meal set. GI of 72 is estimated using the analogous food 'white bread-based fast-food burger bun with refined carbohydrate components' from the University of Sydney GI Database (white bread GI ~70–75; fast-food burger bun analogous estimate ~72). The feast set includes fries and a likely sugary beverage which would further elevate the composite glycaemic load. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: GI (72) × available carbohydrate per serving (207 g carbohydrate in 700 g serving at 29.57 g/100 g) / 100 = 149.1, rounded to 149.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 487 mg/100 g with low potassium offset (197 mg/100 g) and low dietary fibre (1.8 g/100 g); feast format compounds sodium load from fries and sauces
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal set due to its high sodium density (487 mg/100 g), which combined with the fries and sauces in the feast format can easily exceed 2000 mg sodium per sitting; if consumed, request reduced sauce, no added salt on fries, and substitute the sugary beverage with water.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycaemic index (~72) from refined bun, 29.57 g carbohydrate/100 g, low dietary fibre (1.8 g/100 g), and feast format adding fries and likely sugary beverage
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this feast meal set due to its high glycaemic index (~72), high carbohydrate density (29.57 g/100 g), and minimal fibre (1.8 g/100 g), which together produce a very high glycaemic load per serving; if consumed, substitute the sugary beverage with water, skip the pie, and monitor post-meal blood glucose closely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (487 mg/100 g), total fat 10.18 g/100 g from deep-fried components, probable saturated and trans fat from frying, cholesterol 29 mg/100 g, and ultra-processed meal format
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this ultra-processed fast-food feast set due to its high sodium content (487 mg/100 g), significant fat from deep-fried chicken and fries, probable saturated and trans fat exposure, and low dietary fibre, all of which are established risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nightshade ingredients (tomato, chili) in spicy tomato sauce, deep-fried components generating advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), high sodium, and probable trans fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this meal set as the spicy tomato sauce contains nightshade ingredients (tomato and chili) that may exacerbate joint inflammation, the deep-fried chicken and fries generate pro-inflammatory AGEs, and the probable trans fat content is strongly pro-inflammatory at any dose.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Tomato Double Chicken Burger Feast is a Singapore fast-food meal set comprising two deep-fried chicken patties in a refined wheat bun topped with a spicy tomato sauce, served as a complete feast set with medium French fries, a beverage, and a pie. The burger delivers a spicy, savoury flavour profile with a crispy fried chicken texture contrasted by the soft bun and tangy tomato-chili sauce. As an ultra-processed commercial fast-food product sold in Singapore, it is high in refined carbohydrates (29.57 g/100 g), moderate in protein (13.3 g/100 g), and moderate in fat (10.18 g/100 g), with a notably high sodium content of 487 mg/100 g. Dietary fibre is low at 1.8 g/100 g. The feast format substantially increases the total caloric, sodium, and carbohydrate load beyond the burger alone. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 72 (high), making this meal unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The spicy tomato sauce contains nightshade ingredients (tomato and chili) which may be of concern for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. The meal is likely halal-certified in the Singapore market context. Key nutrients per 100 g include energy 263 kcal, protein 13.3 g, fat 10.18 g, carbohydrate 29.57 g, sugar 5.95 g, dietary fibre 1.8 g, sodium 487 mg, potassium 197 mg, calcium 116 mg, magnesium 22 mg, iron 2.87 mg, and cholesterol 29 mg.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values per 100 g are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010116, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food products. The match is exact. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using white bread-based burger bun as the closest approved analogue, which introduces moderate uncertainty for the GI field only; all other fields retain high confidence.
notes: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this commercial feast meal set is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (G00010116) as of 2026-04-16. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this product. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on white bread-based fast-food burger bun from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in any approved source. Halal status is assumed based on Singapore market context but should be verified against the specific restaurant chain's current halal certification. The feast format (burger + fries + drink + pie) means the per-100 g values represent the composite meal set average; individual components will have different nutrient profiles. The health_suitability data from the input record has been incorporated and expanded into the health_context section.
